source_pr.title: executor: get the right result for stmt `select ... for update union select …`
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #31530

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

1. In `executorBuilder` , store `snapshotReadTS` and `forUpdateTS` separately. So every executor can get the right ts according to its own statement.
2.  Introduce `executorBuilder.forDataReaderBuilder` to indicate wether this builder is used by `dataReaderBuilder` . If it is true, the `getSnapshotTS` for executorBuilder is thread safe. This may be a better fix for #30468

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
`select ... for update union select` . The second query will use snapshot read ts instead of for update ts
```

symptoms: - // A quick fix for avoiding a race mentioned in issue #30468. | tk.MustQuery("(select 'a' as c, id, v from t1 for update) union all (select 'b', id, v from t1) order by c").Check(testkit.Rows("a 1 11", "b 1 10")) (executor/executor_test.go)
failure_signature: - // A quick fix for avoiding a race mentioned in issue #30468. | tk.MustQuery("(select 'a' as c, id, v from t1 for update) union all (select 'b', id, v from t1) order by c").Check(testkit.Rows("a 1 11", "b 1 10")) (executor/executor_test.go)
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: - // A quick fix for avoiding a race mentioned in issue #30468. | tk.MustQuery("(select 'a' as c, id, v from t1 for update) union all (select 'b', id, v from t1) order by c").Check(testkit.Rows("a 1 11", "b 1 10")) (executor/executor_test.go)
files: executor/benchmark_test.go, executor/builder.go, executor/executor_test.go, executor/index_lookup_hash_join.go, executor/index_lookup_join.go, executor/index_lookup_merge_join.go
